classification.rationale: The context.md explicitly declares Type: research. The goal is to investigate and prioritize improvements to an existing exoplanet transit detection pipeline, producing a research document — not code changes.

codebase_snapshot.summary: Python 3.10+ exoplanet transit detection pipeline using lightkurve, astropy, and transitleastsquares. Has 16 source files across ingest, preprocessing, BLS/TLS search, vetting, crossmatching, and batch orchestration modules. 9 test files (138 tests) using pytest. Existing capabilities: TESS SPOC ingestion, SavGol flattening, TLS/BLS search, iterative masking, candidate vetting, NASA archive crossmatching, and batch processing with resume.

issues.description: Comparison matrix is wide and may be hard to read in markdown.
Some runtime estimates use ranges without specifying conditions.
Methodology section could cite specific documentation URLs.

verdict.summary: Comprehensive research covering all 12 improvement areas with actionable recommendations. Well-structured findings with maturity and compatibility assessments. Minor readability issues.

issues.description: Chain Overview shows dash for summary agent's own critic verdict.
Next Steps table could include effort estimates.

verdict.summary: Clear executive summary accurately representing research findings. All artifacts referenced, decisions documented, risks consolidated.
